title: Route Merchant

idea.hook: Draw rail lines between cargo stations, route colored crates to the right buyers, and upgrade bottleneck hubs into a humming logistics empire.
idea.hook_ru: Стройте рельсы между грузовыми станциями, развозите цветные ящики нужным покупателям и прокачивайте узкие места в гудящую логистическую империю.
idea.mechanic: Real-time logistics-network builder: the player drags rails between fixed stations (factories emit colored crates, markets demand specific colors, warehouses buffer, junctions auto-route). Crates flow along player-drawn rails in real time; matching deliveries pay coins. Pressure is congestion (a station whose buffer overflows JAMS and bleeds coins) plus unmet buyer patience and a per-stage profit target - NOT a countdown timer. Coins buy upgrades (faster conveyors, bigger buffers, wider market demand). Each stage grows the map and introduces new content (a new crate color, a warehouse, a junction, a demand spike) so the loop escalates across ~10 minutes. Bankruptcy = lose; clearing 8 stages = win then endless freebuild.
idea.core_loop: connect rail -> crate flows -> matching delivery pays -> congestion builds -> re-route / buy upgrade -> hit stage profit target -> map grows + new mechanic unlocks

non_timer_pressure: resource scarcity (finite station buffer capacity -> jams that bleed coins) + buyer patience misses + per-stage profit score target

sources.why: Build-Today #1 (Daily Dodge Run) and market notes both stress that simple, readable production/logistics puzzles with a clear difficulty ramp are an under-monopolized, durable genre worth owning.
LiveOps cheatsheet for a top merge/logistics title - signals that economy/upgrade depth + staged content unlocks (the escalation spine of Route Merchant) is what retains in this family.
Digest 'Useful Knowledge': balance/difficulty ramps must be defined before content - Route Merchant externalizes its ramp into STAGE_TARGETS + per-stage content beats.

alternatives: Daily Dodge Run (Build-Today #1) - endless seeded top-down dodger; rejected for this slot because it is reflex-only and the factory wanted a HIGH-retention-ceiling sim for the Phaser A/B slot.
Tower Balance (Build-Today #2) - physics block-stacking; viable but closer to existing arcade physics games and lower retention ceiling than a logistics economy.
Word Sweep (Build-Today #3) - dictionary swipe puzzle; strong localization but a thinner long-session loop than a growing network.
Conveyor Sushi - a single-belt sushi sorter; simpler but less of a build-your-own-network catch.
Pipe Pressure - fluid-routing puzzle; mechanically adjacent but turn-based and less alive than real-time crate flow.

loop_design.escalation: new station type / crate color / demand spike each stage (~1-2 min): stage1 connect+deliver, stage2 2nd color+market, stage3 warehouse buffer, stage4 demand spike, stage5 junction auto-router + 3rd color, stage6-7 4th color + multi-spike, stage8 peak throughput -> endless freebuild
loop_design.in_session_progression: coins economy -> conveyor/buffer/market upgrades + map growth + stage 1->8
